summary:
Agent1 identified the mylohyoid muscle as crucial for distinguishing the boundaries between the sublingual and submandibular spaces based on extensive literature supporting it being superiorly located relative to both areas. This muscular boundary effectively separates them without involving other structures directly mentioned such as glands or parts of bones.
The primary distinction lies at the level where the mylohyoid muscle sits above connecting tissues defining borders uniquely between those two named spatial locations under discussion concerning regional anatomy specifics highlighted across medical texts referenced herein. formatted_summary:
agent1 (Round 1 summarized): Agent1 identified the mylohyoid muscle as crucial for distinguishing the boundaries between the sublingual and submandibular spaces based on extensive literature supporting it being superiorly located relative to both areas. This muscular boundary effectively separates them without involving other structures directly mentioned such as glands or parts of bones.
The primary distinction lies at the level where the mylohyoid muscle sits above connecting tissues defining borders uniquely between those two named spatial locations under discussion concerning regional anatomy specifics highlighted across medical texts referenced herein.
